title: Using skopen in github actions
body:
I have a [very simple action](https://github.com/lburgazzoli/lb-actions-playground/blob/master/.github/workflows/playground.yml) to test how to use skopeo in a GitHub Action

```yaml
name: run
on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - master
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  kustomize:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - name: "Skopeo"
        run: |    
          skopeo --version
          skopeo inspect docker://busybox:latest
```

When running the action, skopeo fails with the following error:

```
time="2022-05-25T21:0[7](https://github.com/lburgazzoli/lb-actions-playground/runs/6600214480?check_suite_focus=true#step:2:8):37Z" 
level=fatal 
msg="Error parsing image name \"docker://busybox:latest\": getting username and password: 1 error occurred:\n\t* reading JSON file \"/run/containers/1001/auth.json\": open /run/containers/1001/auth.json: permission denied\n\n"
```

Note that the version provided by the runner is  1.4.1

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Basically unprivileged users outside of systemd-managed environments, including minimal containers, need to use `--authfile` to explicitly point at something.

Compare https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/823 / https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/259 .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lburgazzoli @mtrmac  hey I wanted to ask, how can I install skopeo to run in github workflow

@vignesh-gupta-xom There are published Skopeo containers (https://github.com/containers/skopeo/tree/main/contrib/skopeoimage ) , but right now the Skopeo upstream doesn’t maintain a GitHub action container.
